subject: Product Management
title: How Amazon Prioritizes
description: A quick win: understand How Amazon Prioritizes.

hook.text: At Amazon, every new initiative starts with a six-page narrative. If the narrative doesn't convince, the project doesn't start.

buildup.text: The narrative must include: customer problem, proposed solution, FAQs, and a mock press release for the finished product.

discovery.text: Meetings begin with 30 minutes of silent reading. No slides, no presentations.

twist.text: The six-page format forces rigorous thinking. Writing clearly about an idea reveals holes that slides and bullet points hide.

climax.text: Amazon's method means only well-thought-out ideas survive to become priorities.

punchline.text: If you can't write it clearly, you haven't thought it through.

quiz.question: Why does Amazon use six-page narratives instead of slides?
options: Writing forces rigorous thinking that reveals holes slides and bullet points hide
Amazon doesn't have access to presentation software
Narratives are faster to prepare than slides
